prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `1c0d0a4b`.
codex_response:
Rule: full-zero rows and columns are separators; each enclosed `3x3` block is color-complemented so in-block `8 -> 0` and in-block `0 -> 2`, while the separator lines stay `0`.

I implemented the task package in [__init__.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1c0d0a4b/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1c0d0a4b/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1c0d0a4b/generator.py#L4), and [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1c0d0a4b/notes.md). I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint and rejected the Sonnet XOR/parity summary; that mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1c0d0a4b/notes.md). No new shared primitive was needed.

The generator uses a curated pool of official-style `3x3` cyan glyph motifs, expands it with symmetry variants, keeps the observed block layouts, and explicitly rejects samples that would create accidental extra separator frontiers, so the outputs stay visually in-family. Verification passed on `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1c0d0a4b/task.json), [originals.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1c0d0a4b/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1c0d0a4b/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1c0d0a4b/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1c0d0a4b/previews/preview_03.png).
